# Alkamanas UI Full Agent Notes

Use Alkamanas UI when building React web interfaces that need glass surfaces, premium motion, section-level light/dark behavior and shadcn-compatible primitive APIs.

Theme scopes:

```tsx
<section className="alka-theme-dark" data-navbar-theme="dark" data-theme-color="#05060a" />
<section className="alka-theme-light" data-navbar-theme="light" data-theme-color="#f5f5f7" />
<section data-section-theme="dark" />
```

Components inherit tokens from the closest `.alka-theme-*`, `data-theme`, `data-section-theme` or `data-navbar-theme` scope. Unmarked sections fall back to the global app theme.

Navbar:

- `Navbar` scans `[data-navbar-theme]` sections. `SectionAwareNavbar` remains only as a backwards-compatible alias.
- It uses the Visetra Web mobile menu timing and height reveal pattern.
- It can sync browser `theme-color` metadata through `syncThemeMeta`.
- It supports `logo` variants for `wide` and `compact`, each with optional `light` and `dark` alternatives.
- Use `NavbarCTA`, `NavbarMenuItem` and `NavbarMenuSecondaryItem` for composed actions and menus.
- Prefer `import { Navbar } from "@alkamanas/ui/navbar"` and `import "@alkamanas/ui/navbar.css"` for consumer apps.

Package exports:

- Every public component should have a JS/DTS subpath and matching CSS subpath, for example `@alkamanas/ui/button` and `@alkamanas/ui/button.css`.
- `@alkamanas/ui/styles.css` is the full-library stylesheet; focused component CSS is safer for host apps that already own Tailwind/theme globals.
- Package CSS must use `.alka-theme-light` and `.alka-theme-dark`. Avoid adding new generic `.theme-light` or `.theme-dark` selectors to published CSS.
- `react-hook-form` is optional and isolated to `@alkamanas/ui/form`.
- `lucide-react` is optional with peer range `>=0.469.0 <1.0.0`.

Flip cards:

- `FlipCard` preserves the Visetra Web 3D rotateY card structure.
- Prefer concise front content and richer back content.
- Use dark section demos to verify contrast.

Registry:

- Primitive components are available as package exports.
- Registry metadata includes dependencies, tokens and source paths. Run `pnpm registry:update` only when intentionally accepting broad registry hash/token drift.
